title: viburnaceae
intro: The Viburnaceae rank, also known as the honeysuckle family, is a taxonomic rank that consists of a group of flowering plants commonly found in various parts of the world. This rank contains about 750 species of shrubs and small trees, characterized by their showy flowers, often with a fragrance, and red, blue, or white berries. The plants within this family are often deciduous or evergreen, and they can be found in a wide range of environments, from forests to grasslands. The viburnaceae family is known for its ornamental value, with many species being cultivated for their attractive flowers, foliage, and berries.
